title: rx_callback user_data update order allows mismatched callback invocation
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:523-534 (lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback)

Risk: The function updates rx_callback_user_data before rx_callback (lines 531-532). While both are under mutex, the RX thread snapshots both under mutex at lines 219-222. However, the update order creates a subtle issue:

If a caller does:
  set_rx_callback(new_cb, new_data);
  
And the RX thread is between line 220 and 221 (has snapshotted cb but not cb_user_data), it will call the OLD callback with OLD user_data, which is fine.

But the comment says this is to ensure atomic update - it would be clearer to note that the mutex ensures atomicity, not the order. The current order is actually safe, but a contributor might think the order matters and change it to callback-first, which would still be safe due to mutex but might confuse readers.

Pattern contributors may misunderstand: Thinking the write order provides atomicity rather than the mutex.

Suggested fix: Clarify in comment that mutex provides atomicity; write order is arbitrary.
close_reason: Not a real risk - mutex ensures atomicity as code already states. Comment is adequate.
